repo_prior_art.cite: stat_conformal_overlap_frontier/v1: invalid multi-marginal conformal frontier, not a single-selector orbit certificate

published_prior_art.cite: DobribanYu2025 SymmPI: finite-group-symmetry predictive inference with equivariant transformations; WangLiYu2026: finite-sample CRT causal prediction under simple randomization

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_conformal_overlap_frontier/v1: no restricted-support selector-mixture LP linked to causal rank inversion

published_prior_art.cite: RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023: exact randomized permutation tests over arbitrary permutation distributions; DobribanYu2025: group-symmetry prediction coverage
BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024: matched-pair cluster randomization and within-pair randomization tests
BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023: robustness bounds beyond exchangeability; DobribanYu2025: group-symmetry prediction under distribution shift

novelty_flags.label: Theorems 1–2 / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses DobribanYu2025 (SymmPI, JRSSB), a closer published group-symmetry predictive-inference comparator than the test-only HemerikGoeman2018 anchor.

soundness_flags.label: thm:lp-and-rank-inversion
soundness_flags.one_line: The LP is declared separately by arm but the interval uses one common w: separate feasibility can hold with distinct w_0 and w_1 while no common w balances both arms, so the stated joint treatment-effect coverage conclusion does not follow.

soundness_flags.label: def:rank-inversion and target estimand
soundness_flags.one_line: r_{a,(k)} is undefined when k=M+1 because only M calibration scores are ordered, and arbitrary arm-specific q_tar,a over states u=(b,j,a) do not specify the common target unit U whose two potential outcomes define tau_U.

soundness_flags.label: ass:bounded-potential-outcomes
soundness_flags.one_line: Bounded potential outcomes may be a useful converse envelope, but it is not the cited paper's standard conformal-score condition and should be retagged as a separately justified technical condition.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All exact-coverage statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Coverage is conditional on independent calibration block arrays and an independently sampled target block, not on one fixed finite population of dependent matched blocks.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Finite-sample coverage only under the stated independent-replicated-block sampling model.

source_verification_receipts.version: Statistica Sinica accepted-author preprint SS-2025-0476, dated 2026
source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 7–8, Assumptions 1–3; pp. 34–35, concluding future-work paragraph
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper assumes random cluster sampling, Bernoulli simple cluster randomization, and within-cluster exchangeability; it contains no bounded-outcome assumption and explicitly identifies covariate-adaptive randomization such as pair matching as future work.
The paper establishes level control for permutation tests from finite-group invariance and explains why group structure is fundamental; it is a testing, not causal-prediction, result.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Section 4.1, SymmPI construction and distributional-equivariance definition
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: SymmPI gives predictive inference under general group symmetries using distributionally equivariant transformations and valid coverage under distributional invariance.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the target as a base member T=(b,j), index arm-specific observed states separately, impose either a single joint selector constraint or explicitly use (w_0,w_1), and define r_{a,(M+1)}=infinity; then restate coverage for the same T.
State and prove the simultaneous-selector feasibility theorem with a concrete two-arm counterexample showing why marginal LP feasibility is insufficient.
Add SymmPI as the principal symmetry-prediction comparator and sharpen the surviving novelty to a necessity theorem for two selector measures plus a genuinely nontrivial common-selector certificate.

recommended_next_step: Reject this malformed core and redraft its common-target/common-selector construction before a fresh novelty review.
